title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
constraints: k is in the range [1, the number of unique elements in nums]
It is guaranteed that the answer is unique

hint_plan.level_1: Break it into counting, then selecting the top items by frequency.
hint_plan.level_2: Frequency map plus a heap or bucket-by-frequency often works well.
hint_plan.level_3: Count occurrences, then pick k keys with largest counts (heap nlargest or bucket sort by freq).
hint_plan.level_4: Build Counter(nums); use heapq.nlargest(k, counter.keys(), key=counter.get) or bucket arrays indexed by frequency.

canonical_solution_summary: Count frequencies, then select k largest by frequency via heap or bucket sort.
